corpus: live dev server, /api/conversations/a70251a5-b932-4b61-aba1-16a70410b98e (21114 messages → 4051 visible bubbles → ~141K DOM nodes)
harness: Playwright MCP, evaluate() in real Chromium against http://localhost:5173, real backend on :8765

search_typing_this_image_on_LARGE.comment: Down from prior baseline 'keystrokes landing ~17s apart' to ~240ms p95. Prior fix (active-match-only highlight) shipped at commit 3cdfcd1/c913c24.

filter_drift_repro_attempt.verdict: DOES NOT REPRO on this corpus. All 32 compact markers ARE present in keyboard-nav messages. computeVisibleMessages includes compact markers via the carve-out; messageHasVisibleContent ALSO returns true for them because they have summary text content. The bug is LATENT — would manifest only when a compact-marker message lacks text/visible-content (e.g., a tool-only message marked as compact). Fix is still correct prophylactically per Task 2; just is not the dominant pain source.

summary.dominant_pain_source: RENDER. Cold-vs-warm delta is only ~2.3s (fetch + queryClient hydration). The remaining ~10s is React reconciliation + 4051 MessageBubble first-mount + ~141K DOM node creation. Warm switch-back is barely faster than cold because the entire detail subtree unmounts on route change (no keep-alive) and re-mounts from scratch.
implications_for_ABC_plan: Option A (Memo/render-cost optimization on MessageBubble): targets the right phase (render) but each mount is a fresh fiber tree so memo doesn't help on FIRST mount. Could help on subsequent re-renders post-mount.
Option B (Virtualization via @tanstack/react-virtual or similar): targets the right phase + addresses the 141K DOM node problem directly. Likely 10x win on cold + warm.
Option C (Route-level keep-alive / cached subtree): targets warm-switch-back specifically. Would drop warm from 10s to <100ms. Cold unaffected.

Order recommendation: B first (largest win, also fixes cold), then C for warm-switch instant-feel polish. A becomes lower priority — the per-row work isn't the dominant cost; the SHEER NUMBER of mounted rows is.
